types.name: CustomEmoji

fields.doc: A link to a static copy of the custom emoji.

types.name: FeatureApproval
types.doc: Summary of an account's policy with regards to being featured in a Collection and how it applies to the requesting user.

fields.name: automatic
fields.doc: Describes who is expected to be able to feature the account in a Collection and have this authorized automatically. An empty list means that nobody is expected to be able to feature this account with automatic approval. Other values may be added in the future, so unknown values should be treated as `unsupported_policy`.

fields.name: current_user
fields.doc: Describes how this account's feature approval policy applies to the current user.

fields.name: manual
fields.doc: Describes who is expected to have attempts of featuring this account in a Collection be manually reviewed by the account owner before being accepted. An empty list means that nobody is expected to be able to feature this account with manual approval. Other values may be added in the future, so unknown values should be treated as `unsupported_policy`.

types.doc: Additional entity definition for Field

fields.doc: The key of a given field's key-value pair. This is the raw string before processing, not HTML.

fields.name: value

fields.name: verified_at

types.name: Account
types.doc: Represents a user of Mastodon and their associated profile.

fields.name: acct
fields.doc: The WebFinger account URI. Equal to `username` for local users, or `username@domain` for remote users.

fields.name: avatar
fields.doc: An image icon that is shown next to statuses and in the profile.

fields.name: avatar_static
fields.doc: A static version of the avatar. Equal to `avatar` if its value is a static image; different if `avatar` is an animated GIF.

fields.name: bot
fields.doc: Indicates that the account may perform automated actions, may not be monitored, or identifies as a robot. This is determined by the account's `actor_type` being set to 'Application' or 'Service'.

fields.name: created_at
fields.doc: When the account was created.

fields.name: display_name
fields.doc: The profile's display name.

fields.name: emojis
fields.doc: Custom emoji entities to be used when rendering the profile.

fields.name: fields
fields.doc: Additional metadata attached to a profile as name-value pairs.

fields.name: followers_count
fields.doc: The reported followers of this profile.

fields.name: following_count
fields.doc: The reported follows of this profile.

fields.name: group
fields.doc: Indicates that the account represents a Group actor.

fields.name: header
fields.doc: An image banner that is shown above the profile and in profile cards. Will end `/headers/original/missing.png` if the user has not set a header image.

fields.name: header_static
fields.doc: A static version of the header. Equal to `header` if its value is a static image; different if `header` is an animated GIF.

fields.name: id
fields.doc: The account id.

fields.name: locked
fields.doc: Whether the account manually approves follow requests.

fields.name: note
fields.doc: The profile's bio or description.

fields.name: statuses_count
fields.doc: How many statuses are attached to this account.

fields.name: uri
fields.doc: The user's ActivityPub actor identifier (used for federation).

fields.name: username
fields.doc: The username of the account, not including domain.

fields.name: avatar_description
fields.doc: A textual description of the avatar image.

fields.name: discoverable
fields.doc: Whether the account has opted into discovery features such as the profile directory.

fields.name: feature_approval
fields.doc: Summary of the account's policy with regards to being featured in a [Collection]({{< relref "entities/Collection" >}}) and how it applies to the user making the request.

fields.name: header_description
fields.doc: A textual description of the header image.

fields.name: hide_collections
fields.doc: Whether the user hides the contents of their follows and followers collections.

fields.name: indexable
fields.doc: Whether the account allows indexing by search engines.

fields.name: last_status_at
fields.doc: When the most recent status was posted.

fields.name: limited
fields.doc: An extra attribute returned only when an account is silenced. If true, indicates that the account should be hidden behind a warning screen.

fields.name: memorial
fields.doc: An extra attribute returned only when an account is memorialized (when `memorial` is true).

fields.name: moved
fields.doc: Indicates that the profile is currently inactive and that its user has moved to a new account.

fields.name: noindex
fields.doc: Whether the local user has opted out of being indexed by search engines.

fields.name: roles
fields.doc: An array of roles assigned to the user that are publicly visible (highlighted roles only), if the account is local. Will be an empty array if no roles are highlighted or null if the account is remote.

fields.name: show_featured
fields.doc: Whether the account wishes to have a “Featured” tab on their profile.

fields.name: show_media
fields.doc: Whether the account wishes to have a “Media” tab with media attachments on their profile.

fields.name: show_media_replies
fields.doc: Whether the account wishes to have replies in the “Media” tab on their profile.

fields.name: suspended
fields.doc: An extra attribute returned only when an account is suspended.

fields.name: url
fields.doc: The location of the user's profile page (web interface URL).

fields.name: discoverable
fields.doc: Whether the account has opted into discovery features such as the profile directory.

fields.name: hide_collections
fields.doc: Whether the user hides the contents of their follows and followers collections.

fields.name: indexable
fields.doc: Whether public posts should be searchable to anyone.

fields.name: language

fields.name: quote_policy
fields.doc: The default quote policy to be used for new statuses.

types.name: Role
types.doc: Represents a custom user role that grants permissions.

fields.name: color
fields.doc: The hex code assigned to this role. If no hex code is assigned, the string will be empty.

fields.name: highlighted
fields.doc: Whether the role is publicly visible as a badge on user profiles.

fields.doc: The ID of the Role in the database.

fields.doc: The name of the role.

fields.name: permissions
fields.doc: A bitmask that represents the sum of all permissions granted to the role. This is a potentially large integer in decimal representation. The absence of special permissions is denoted by `'0'`.

fields.name: collection_limit
fields.doc: Maximum number of Collections that users with this role are allowed to create.

types.name: Appeal
types.doc: Appeal against a moderation action.

fields.name: state
fields.doc: State of the appeal.

fields.name: text
fields.doc: Text of the appeal from the moderated account to the moderators.

types.name: AccountWarning
types.doc: Moderation warning against a particular account.

fields.name: action
fields.doc: Action taken against the account.

fields.name: created_at
fields.doc: When the event took place.

fields.name: id
fields.doc: The ID of the account warning.

fields.name: target_account
fields.doc: Account against which a moderation decision has been taken. If this `AccountWarning` is present in a [Notification](/entities/Notification/) then this is always the same as the authenticated account that requested the notification.

fields.name: text
fields.doc: Message from the moderator to the target account.

fields.name: appeal
fields.doc: Appeal submitted by the target account, if any.

fields.name: status_ids
fields.doc: List of status IDs that are relevant to the warning. When `action` is `mark_statuses_as_sensitive` or `delete_statuses`, those are the affected statuses. If the action is `delete_statuses` then they have been irrevocably deleted (irrespective of the appeal state), and will be inaccessible to the client.

fields.name: locale

fields.name: role

fields.name: ip
fields.doc: The IP address last used to login to this account.

fields.doc: The numeric total associated with the requested measure, in the previous period. Previous period is calculated by subtracting the start_at and end_at dates, then offsetting both start and end dates backwards by the length of the time period.

fields.name: unit

fields.doc: The contexts in which the filter should be applied.

fields.name: id
fields.doc: The ID of the filter in the database.

fields.name: irreversible
fields.doc: Should matching entities in home and notifications be dropped by the server? See [implementation guidelines for filters]({{< relref "api/guidelines#filters" >}}).

fields.name: phrase
fields.doc: The text to be filtered.

fields.name: whole_word
fields.doc: Should the filter consider word boundaries? See [implementation guidelines for filters]({{< relref "api/guidelines#filters" >}}).

fields.name: expires_at
fields.doc: When the filter should no longer be applied.

types.name: NotificationGroup
types.doc: ### Attributes

fields.name: group_key
fields.doc: Group key identifying the grouped notifications. Should be treated as an opaque value.

fields.name: most_recent_notification_id
fields.doc: ID of the most recent notification in the group.

fields.name: notifications_count
fields.doc: Total number of individual notifications that are part of this notification group.

fields.name: sample_account_ids
fields.doc: IDs of some of the accounts who most recently triggered notifications in this group.

fields.name: type
fields.doc: The type of event that resulted in the notifications in this group.

fields.name: event
fields.doc: Summary of the event that caused follow relationships to be severed. Attached when `type` of the notification is `severed_relationships`.

fields.name: fallback
fields.doc: Fallback information available for some notification types that clients may not support. Only available for some notification types, and only if the `supported_types` parameter is used when querying.

fields.name: latest_page_notification_at
fields.doc: Date at which the most recent notification from this group within the current page has been created. This is only returned when paginating through notification groups.

fields.name: moderation_warning
fields.doc: Moderation warning that caused the notification. Attached when `type` of the notification is `moderation_warning`.

fields.name: page_max_id
fields.doc: ID of the newest notification from this group represented within the current page. This is only returned when paginating through notification groups. Useful when polling new notifications.

fields.name: page_min_id
fields.doc: ID of the oldest notification from this group represented within the current page. This is only returned when paginating through notification groups. Useful when polling new notifications.

fields.name: report
fields.doc: Report that was the object of the notification. Attached when `type` of the notification is `admin.report`.

fields.name: status_id
fields.doc: ID of the [Status]({{< relref "entities/Status" >}}) that was the object of the notification. Attached when `type` of the notification is `favourite`, `reblog`, `status`, `mention`, `poll`, `update`, `quote` or `quoted_update`. In the case of `quoted_update`, your quote of the edited status is attached, not the status that was edited.

types.name: BaseStatus
types.doc: Common fields for all status creation requests

fields.name: in_reply_to_id
fields.doc: ID of the status being replied to, if status is a reply.

fields.name: language
fields.doc: ISO 639-1 language code for this status.

fields.name: quote_approval_policy
fields.doc: String (Enumerable, oneOf). Sets who is allowed to quote the status. When omitted, the user's [default setting] will be used instead. Ignored if `visibility` is `private` or `direct`, in which case the policy will always be set to `nobody`.

fields.name: quoted_status_id
fields.doc:
ID of the status being quoted, if any. Will raise an error if the status does not exist, the author does not have access to it, or quoting is denied by Mastodon's understanding of the attached quote policy. All posts except Private Mentions (`direct` visibility) are quotable by their author. Quoting a `private` post will restrict the quoting post's `visibility` to `private` or `direct` (if the given `visibility` is `public` or `unlisted`, `private` will be used instead). An error will be returned when making a quote post with `direct` visibility and the quote author is not explicitly mentioned. If the `status` text doesn't include a link to the quoted post, Mastodon will prepend a `

RE: …

` paragraph for backward compatibility (such a paragraph will be hidden by Mastodon's web interface).

fields.name: scheduled_at
fields.doc: [Datetime] at which to schedule a status. Providing this parameter will cause ScheduledStatus to be returned instead of Status. Must be at least 5 minutes in the future.

fields.name: sensitive
fields.doc: Mark status and attached media as sensitive? Defaults to false.

fields.name: spoiler_text
fields.doc: Text to be shown as a warning or subject before the actual content. Statuses are generally collapsed behind this field.

fields.name: visibility
fields.doc: Sets the visibility of the posted status to `public`, `unlisted`, `private`, `direct`.
